_meta.title: MARCH-PAWS Canonical Vocabulary, 2026 TCCC Guidelines
primary_sources: tccc guidelines 2026/chapter2/*.html — Tactical Field Care, sections 1-20
_meta.extraction_method: direct verbatim from source HTML, no paraphrase or RAG retrieval
_meta.intended_consumer: TCCCKit Swift extractors — pattern updates for MARCH/PAWS phase recognition
_meta.naming_note: The 2026 guidelines use numeric section labels (3, 4, 5, ...) rather than the literal MARCH-PAWS letter mnemonic. The mapping is: M=3, A=4, R=5, C=6, H=7+8 (Hypothermia + TBI). PAWS does not appear as a literal acronym in the 2026 guidelines but the four phases map to: P=11 (Analgesia), A=12 (Antibiotics), W=13 (Inspect and dress wounds), S=16 (Splints).

summary_of_2026_changes.section_2_triage: removed weapons/comms-removal directive; references new Supplement A — Triage in TCCC
summary_of_2026_changes.section_4_airway_management: FLAGGED AS NEW in chapter structure — substantially revised airway management section
summary_of_2026_changes.section_5_respiration: TBI oxygen saturation target changed from >90% to ≥92%
summary_of_2026_changes.section_6_bleeding: tourniquet REPOSITION (apply second TQ to skin then loosen first) replaces 2024 'replace' language; ASM/CLS personnel should not attempt TQ conversion beyond 2 hours without higher-level direction
summary_of_2026_changes.section_8_tbi: separated mild from moderate/severe; SBP target changed from 100-110 to >100 mmHg; crystalloid bolus replaced with 1-2 units of plasma if no hemorrhage; EtCO2 target 35-45 mmHg or 10 breaths/min if no EtCO2 monitoring
summary_of_2026_changes.section_9_eye: antibiotic changed from moxifloxacin CWMP to ceftriaxone 2g IV/IM or cefadroxil 1g PO
summary_of_2026_changes.section_11_analgesia: CWMP acetaminophen 1000-1300mg q8h; SUZETRIGINE added; OTFC/fentanyl removed; ketamine simplified; esketamine IN added
summary_of_2026_changes.section_12_antibiotics: moxifloxacin/ertapenem replaced with cefadroxil PO or ceftriaxone IV/IO/IM

M.name: Massive Hemorrhage

assessment_findings.verbatim: Assess for unrecognized hemorrhage and control all sources of bleeding.
use a CoTCCC-recommended limb tourniquet to control life-threatening external hemorrhage that is anatomically amenable to tourniquet use
use a CoTCCC-recommended limb tourniquet ... or for any traumatic amputation

assessment_findings.term: compressible hemorrhage not amenable to limb tourniquet
assessment_findings.verbatim: For compressible (external) hemorrhage not amenable to limb tourniquet use or as an adjunct to tourniquet removal, use Combat Gauze
altered mental status in the absence of brain injury and/or weak or absent radial pulse

interventions.name: limb tourniquet
interventions.verbatim: CoTCCC-recommended limb tourniquet
interventions.device_specs: Apply directly to the skin 2-3 inches above the bleeding site. If bleeding is not controlled with the first tourniquet, apply a second tourniquet side-by-side with the first.

interventions.name: Combat Gauze
interventions.verbatim: Combat Gauze as the CoTCCC hemostatic dressing of choice
interventions.device_specs: Apply with at least 3 minutes of direct pressure

interventions.name: XStat
interventions.verbatim: XStat (Best for deep, narrow-tract junctional wounds)
interventions.device_specs: Direct pressure optional; not to be removed in the field

interventions.name: iTClamp
interventions.verbatim: iTClamp (may be used alone or in conjunction with hemostatic dressing or XStat)
interventions.device_specs: Does not require additional direct pressure. DO NOT APPLY on or near the eye or eyelid (within 1cm of the orbit). Used as primary option for head/neck wounds where edges can be re-approximated.

interventions.name: junctional tourniquet
interventions.verbatim: If the bleeding site is amenable to use of a junctional tourniquet, immediately apply a junctional tourniquet
interventions.device_specs: Apply hemostatic dressings with direct pressure if a junctional tourniquet is not available or while the junctional tourniquet is being readied for use

decision_thresholds.threshold: 2-3 inches above the bleeding site, directly to skin
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: Apply directly to the skin 2-3 inches above the bleeding site

decision_thresholds.threshold: side-by-side with the first, if bleeding not controlled
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: If bleeding is not controlled with the first tourniquet, apply a second tourniquet side-by-side with the first
Hemostatic dressings should be applied with at least 3 minutes of direct pressure (optional for XStat)

decision_thresholds.threshold: within 1cm of the orbit — do not apply
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: DO NOT APPLY on or near the eye or eyelid (within 1cm of the orbit)

decision_thresholds.threshold: altered mental status (without brain injury) AND/OR weak or absent radial pulse
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: altered mental status in the absence of brain injury and/or weak or absent radial pulse

documentation_requirements: Mark all tourniquets with time of application using permanent marker
Note tourniquets applied, time of application, time of re-application, time of conversion, and time of removal on TCCC Casualty Card
negation_phrases: not amenable to limb tourniquet use

A.name: Airway Management
A._2026_note: Airway Management section is flagged NEW in the chapter structure. Substantially revised from prior versions.

assessment_findings.verbatim: Assess for unobstructed airway
If there is a traumatic airway obstruction or impending traumatic obstruction, prepare for possible direct airway intervention
the casualty's airway obstruction (e.g. facial fractures, direct airway injury, blood, deformation or burns) is unmanageable

interventions.name: conscious casualty positioning
interventions.verbatim: Allow a conscious casualty to assume any position that best protects the airway, to include sitting up and/or leaning forward

interventions.name: recovery position
interventions.verbatim: Place unconscious casualty in the recovery position, head tilted back; chin away from chest

interventions.name: suction
interventions.verbatim: Use suction if available and appropriate

interventions.name: surgical cricothyroidotomy
interventions.verbatim: perform a surgical cricothyroidotomy
interventions.device_specs: Bougie-aided open or standard open surgical technique using a flanged and cuffed airway cannula of less than 10 mm outer diameter, 6-7 mm internal diameter, and 5-8 cm of intratracheal length. Verify placement with continuous EtCO2 capnography. Use lidocaine if the casualty is conscious.

decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: flanged and cuffed airway cannula of less than 10 mm outer diameter, 6-7 mm internal diameter, and 5-8 cm of intratracheal length

decision_thresholds.criterion: cervical spine stabilization for penetrating trauma
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: Cervical spine stabilization is not necessary for casualties who have sustained only penetrating trauma

assessment_findings.verbatim: significant torso trauma or primary blast injury and one or more of the following: Severe or progressive respiratory distress; Severe or progressive tachypnea; Absent or markedly decreased breath sounds on one side of the chest; Hemoglobin oxygen saturation < 90% on pulse oximetry; Shock; Traumatic cardiac arrest without obviously fatal wounds
All open and/or sucking chest wounds should be treated by immediately applying a vented chest seal
Casualties with moderate/severe TBI should be given supplemental oxygen when available to maintain an oxygen saturation ≥ 92 %

If the casualty has impaired ventilation and uncorrectable hypoxia with decreasing oxygen saturation below 90% (or below 92% with moderate/severe TBI), consider insertion of a properly sized Nasopharyngeal Airway, and ventilate using a 1000ml resuscitator Bag-Valve-Mask

interventions.name: needle decompression (NDC)
interventions.verbatim: Decompress the chest on the side of the injury with a 14-gauge or a 10-gauge, 3.25-inch needle/catheter unit
interventions.device_specs: 5th ICS in AAL or 2nd ICS in MCL. If MCL site, do not insert needle medial to nipple line. Insert at angle perpendicular to chest wall, just over top of lower rib at insertion site. Insert all the way to hub, hold 5-10 seconds. After NDC, remove needle and leave catheter in place.

interventions.name: vented chest seal
interventions.verbatim: applying a vented chest seal to cover the defect
interventions.device_specs: If a vented chest seal is not available, use a non-vented chest seal. Monitor for development of subsequent tension pneumothorax. If casualty develops increasing hypoxia, respiratory distress, or hypotension and tension pneumothorax suspected, treat by burping or removing the dressing or by needle decompression.

interventions.name: bilateral NDC for traumatic cardiac arrest
interventions.verbatim: If a casualty has significant torso trauma or primary blast injury and is in traumatic cardiac arrest (no pulse, no respirations, no response to painful stimuli, no other signs of life), decompress both sides of the chest before discontinuing treatment

interventions.name: pulse oximetry
interventions.verbatim: Initiate pulse oximetry. All individuals with moderate/severe TBI should be monitored with pulse oximetry. Readings may be misleading in the settings of shock or marked hypothermia.

interventions.name: Nasopharyngeal Airway with Bag-Valve-Mask ventilation
interventions.verbatim: consider insertion of a properly sized Nasopharyngeal Airway, and ventilate using a 1000ml resuscitator Bag-Valve-Mask
interventions.device_specs: 1000ml BVM

interventions.name: continuous EtCO2 and SpO2 monitoring
interventions.verbatim: Use continuous EtCO2 and SpO2 monitoring to help assess airway patency

decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: Hemoglobin oxygen saturation < 90% on pulse oximetry
14-gauge or a 10-gauge, 3.25-inch needle/catheter unit
Either the 5th intercostal space (ICS) in the anterior axillary line (AAL) or the 2nd ICS in the mid-clavicular line (MCL)
Insert the needle/catheter unit all the way to the hub and hold it in place for 5-10 seconds
Hemoglobin oxygen saturation increases to 90% or greater
maintain an oxygen saturation ≥ 92 %
decreasing oxygen saturation below 90% (or below 92% with moderate/severe TBI)

C.name: Circulation

assessment_findings.verbatim: Severe blunt force or blast injury with one or more of the following indications: Pelvic pain; Any major lower limb amputation or near amputation; Physical exam findings suggestive of a pelvic fracture; Unconsciousness; Shock
altered mental status in the absence of brain injury and/or weak or absent radial pulse

presents with hemorrhagic shock, one or more major amputations, penetrating torso trauma, or evidence of severe bleeding ... OR If the casualty has signs or symptoms of significant TBI or has altered mental status associated with blast injury or blunt trauma

If a casualty in shock is not responding to fluid resuscitation, consider untreated tension pneumothorax as a possible cause of refractory shock. Thoracic trauma, persistent respiratory distress, absent breath sounds, and hemoglobin oxygen saturation < 90% support this diagnosis.

interventions.name: pelvic binder
interventions.verbatim: A pelvic binder should be applied for cases of suspected pelvic fracture

interventions.name: tourniquet reposition
interventions.verbatim: reposition any limb tourniquet placed over the uniform by applying a second one directly to the skin 2-3 inches above the bleeding site, then loosening the first tourniquet
interventions._2026_change: REPOSITION (apply second TQ first, then loosen first) replaces 2024 'replace' language

interventions.name: tourniquet conversion
interventions.verbatim: Limb tourniquets and junctional tourniquets should be converted to hemostatic or pressure dressings as soon as possible if three criteria are met: the casualty is not in shock; it is possible to monitor the wound closely for bleeding; and the tourniquet is not being used to control bleeding from an amputated extremity.
interventions.device_specs: Convert in less than 2 hours if bleeding can be controlled with other means. Do not remove a tourniquet that has been in place more than 6 hours unless close monitoring and lab capability are available. ASM/CLS personnel should not attempt conversion beyond 2 hours post-application without higher-level direction.

interventions.name: IV/IO access
interventions.verbatim: Intravenous (IV) or intraosseous (IO) access is indicated if the casualty is in hemorrhagic shock or at significant risk of shock
interventions.device_specs: An 18-gauge IV or saline lock is preferred. If vascular access is needed but not quickly obtainable via the IV route, use the IO route.

interventions.name: Tranexamic Acid (TXA)
interventions.verbatim: Administer 2 gm of tranexamic acid via slow IV or IO push as soon as possible but NOT later than 3 hours after injury
interventions.device_specs: 2g via slow IV or IO push

interventions.name: fluid resuscitation — order of preference
interventions.verbatim: 1. Cold stored low titer O whole blood; 2. Pre-screened low titer O fresh whole blood; 3. Plasma, red blood cells (RBCs) and platelets in a 1:1:1 ratio; 4. Plasma and RBCs in a 1:1 ratio; 5. Plasma or RBCs alone
interventions.device_specs: If blood products transfused, administer 1 g calcium (30 ml of 10% calcium gluconate or 10 ml of 10% calcium chloride) IV/IO after first transfused product

interventions.name: calcium administration after transfusion
interventions.verbatim: administer one gram of calcium (30 ml of 10% calcium gluconate or 10 ml of 10% calcium chloride) IV/IO after the first transfused product
interventions.device_specs: 1g calcium IV/IO

decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: the casualty is not in shock

decision_thresholds.threshold: convert in less than 2 hours; do not remove if more than 6 hours unless close monitoring + lab
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: Every effort should be made to convert tourniquets in less than 2 hours if bleeding can be controlled with other means. Do not remove a tourniquet that has been in place more than 6 hours unless close monitoring and lab capability are available.

decision_thresholds.threshold: should not attempt conversion beyond 2 hours post-application without higher-level direction
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: TCCC ASM/CLS trained personnel, should not attempt tourniquet conversion beyond 2 hours post-application unless directed by TCCC CMC/CPP personnel or other advanced medical personnel

decision_thresholds.threshold: as soon as possible but NOT later than 3 hours after injury
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: Administer 2 gm of tranexamic acid via slow IV or IO push as soon as possible but NOT later than 3 hours after injury

decision_thresholds.threshold: palpable radial pulse, improved mental status, or systolic BP of 100 mmHg
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: Continue resuscitation until a palpable radial pulse, improved mental status or systolic BP of 100 mmHg is present

If a casualty with an altered mental status due to suspected TBI has a weak or absent radial pulse, resuscitate as necessary to restore and maintain a normal radial pulse. If BP monitoring is available, maintain a target systolic BP greater than 100 mmHg.
An 18-gauge IV or saline lock is preferred

documentation_requirements: Note time of TQ removal on TCCC Casualty Card if reassessment determines prior TQ not needed
Mark all TQs with time of application using permanent marker on TQ AND casualty card
Note: time of application; time of re-application; time of conversion; time of removal
negation_phrases: no IV fluids are immediately necessary

H_hypothermia.name: Hypothermia Prevention
H_hypothermia._note: MARCH H is hypothermia prevention. The 2026 guidelines treat TBI as a separate section (8) but it shares the H letter in the MARCH-PAWS mnemonic for some training materials. Both phases captured here; TBI is in H_tbi key.

assessment_findings.term: trauma or burn casualty heat loss risk
assessment_findings.verbatim: Take early and aggressive steps to prevent further body heat loss and add external heat, when possible, for both trauma and severely burned casualties

interventions.name: minimize cold exposure
interventions.verbatim: Minimize casualty's exposure to cold ground, wind and air temperatures. Place insulation material between the casualty and any cold surface as soon as possible. Keep protective gear on or with the casualty if feasible.

interventions.name: replace wet clothing
interventions.verbatim: Replace wet clothing with dry clothing, if possible, and protect from further heat loss

interventions.name: active heating blanket placement
interventions.verbatim: Place an active heating blanket on the casualty's anterior torso and under the arms in the axillae
interventions.device_specs: To prevent burns, do not place any active heating source directly on the skin or wrap around the torso

interventions.name: exterior impermeable enclosure bag
interventions.verbatim: Enclose the casualty with the exterior impermeable enclosure bag

interventions.name: insulated enclosure system upgrade
interventions.verbatim: upgrade hypothermia enclosure system to a well-insulated enclosure system using a hooded sleeping bag or other readily available insulation inside the enclosure bag/external vapor barrier shell

interventions.name: battery-powered fluid warmer
interventions.verbatim: Use a battery-powered warming device to deliver IV/IO resuscitation fluids, in accordance with current CoTCCC guidelines, at flow rate up to 150 ml/min with a 38°C output temperature
interventions.device_specs: Up to 150 ml/min, 38°C output temp

interventions.name: evacuation platform wind/precip protection
interventions.verbatim: Protect the casualty from exposure to wind and precipitation on any evacuation platform

decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: flow rate up to 150 ml/min
38°C output temperature

H_tbi.name: Traumatic Brain Injury
H_tbi._note: Distinct section in 2026 guidelines. Substantially revised in 2026.
_2026_changes: separated mild TBI/concussion from moderate/severe TBI
moderate/severe TBI defined as inability to follow simple instructions beyond 10 minutes post injury with suspected head injury and no alternative cause
evacuation to neurosurgical capability ideally within 5 hours
SBP target changed from 100-110 mmHg to >100 mmHg or normal radial pulse
crystalloid bolus replaced with 1-2 units of plasma if no hemorrhage
EtCO2 target 35-45 mmHg, or 10 breaths/min if EtCO2 monitoring unavailable
added detailed penetrating TBI / open skull fracture care; clarified that these are NOT automatically expectant

assessment_findings.verbatim: Unable to follow simple instructions (thumbs up, two fingers or blink) beyond 10 minutes post injury AND suspected head injury without alternative cause
Evaluation and treatment of suspected mild TBI/concussion is generally best deferred to Prolonged Casualty Care or subsequent phases of care
asymmetric or fixed/dilated pupil(s), or posturing

interventions.name: supplemental oxygen for TBI
interventions.verbatim: Prevent hypoxemia (goal oxygen saturation ≥ 92%) and provide airway and respiratory interventions per TCCC Guidelines in Section (4) and (5). Administer supplemental oxygen if available.

interventions.name: BP management for TBI
interventions.verbatim: Prevent hypotension and target a systolic blood pressure greater than 100 mmHg OR, if measurement capability is absent, target a normal radial pulse

interventions.name: plasma for isolated TBI
interventions.verbatim: If no evidence of hemorrhage, administer 1-2 units of plasma. Plasma is not indicated for mild TBI /concussion.
interventions.device_specs: 1-2 units plasma

interventions.name: ventilation targeting EtCO2
interventions.verbatim: If patient is ventilated and end-tidal CO2 monitoring is available, target an EtCO2 of 35-45 mmHg. If ventilating a patient and EtCO2 monitoring not available, provide low tidal volume ventilation at 10 breaths per minute (one breath every 6 seconds).
interventions.device_specs: EtCO2 35-45 mmHg, or 10 breaths/min (1 breath / 6 sec)

interventions.name: elevate head and torso
interventions.verbatim: Elevate head and torso greater than 30 degrees IF casualty not in shock AND tactically feasible
interventions.device_specs: >30 degrees

interventions.name: loosen cervical collar
interventions.verbatim: Loosen cervical collar if present and keep head facing forward without rotation of the neck

interventions.name: hypertonic saline for herniation
interventions.verbatim: Administer 250ml of 3% or 5% hypertonic saline OR 30ml of 23.4% hypertonic saline IV/IO over at least 10 minutes followed by a saline flush
interventions.device_specs: 250ml of 3% or 5% saline, OR 30ml of 23.4% saline; over ≥10 minutes; max 2 doses, repeat in 20 min if no response
warnings: Do NOT attempt to use hypertonic saline prophylactically
Hypertonic saline is NOT a resuscitative fluid

decision_thresholds.threshold: unable to follow simple instructions beyond 10 minutes post injury
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: Unable to follow simple instructions (thumbs up, two fingers or blink) beyond 10 minutes post injury

decision_thresholds.threshold: neurosurgical capability ideally within 5 hours of injury
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: outcomes are improved with surgical intervention within 5 hours of injury
Prevent hypoxemia (goal oxygen saturation ≥ 92%)
target a systolic blood pressure greater than 100 mmHg
target an EtCO2 of 35-45 mmHg

decision_thresholds.threshold: 10 breaths per minute (1 breath every 6 seconds)
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: low tidal volume ventilation at 10 breaths per minute (one breath every 6 seconds)
Elevate head and torso greater than 30 degrees

decision_thresholds.threshold: max 2 doses; repeat at 20 min if no response
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: Repeat in 20 minutes if no response (max 2 doses)

documentation_requirements: Reassess neurologic status every 5-10 minutes to evaluate for worsening status

PAWS._note: The 2026 guidelines do NOT use 'PAWS' as a literal acronym. The four phases below are mapped from 2026 sections 11, 12, 13, and 16 to align with TCCC training-material PAWS mnemonic conventions.

P.name: Analgesia
_2026_changes: CWMP acetaminophen dosing changed to 1000-1300 mg every 8 hours
Suzetrigine ADDED to the CWMP regimen
OTFC and fentanyl options REMOVED for initial management
Procedural sedation text moved to inspect/dress wounds section

assessment_findings.verbatim: Casualty can stay in the fight/mission capable
Casualty cannot stay in the fight/non-mission capable

interventions.name: TCCC Combat Wound Medication Pack (CWMP)
interventions.verbatim: TCCC Combat Wound Medication Pack (CWMP)
interventions.device_specs: Self-administered or co-administered. Components: Acetaminophen 1000-1300mg PO q8h; Meloxicam 15mg PO QD; Suzetrigine 100mg PO once (2x50mg) then 50mg PO q12h

interventions.name: Acetaminophen
interventions.verbatim: Acetaminophen – 1000mg - 1300mg (e.g., two 650mg extended-release caplets) PO every 8 hours

interventions.name: Meloxicam
interventions.verbatim: Meloxicam – 15 mg PO once a day
interventions.device_specs: 15mg PO QD

interventions.name: Suzetrigine
interventions.verbatim: Suzetrigine – 100 mg PO once (2 x 50 mg tablets) then 50 mg PO every 12 hours
interventions.device_specs: 100mg PO load, then 50mg PO q12h

interventions.name: Ketamine IM
interventions.verbatim: Ketamine 100 mg IM
interventions.device_specs: 100mg IM

interventions.name: Ketamine IN
interventions.verbatim: Ketamine 50 mg IN
interventions.device_specs: 50mg IN. Use higher concentration (100 mg/ml) for IN route to minimize volume.

interventions.name: Ketamine IV/IO
interventions.verbatim: Ketamine 25 mg (or 0.2 - 0.3 mg/kg) delivered IV or IO over 1 minute
interventions.device_specs: 25mg or 0.2-0.3 mg/kg IV/IO, slow over 1 min

interventions.name: Esketamine IN
interventions.verbatim: Esketamine 14 or 28 mg IN x 1
interventions.device_specs: 14 or 28mg IN, single dose

interventions.name: Ondansetron
interventions.verbatim: Ondansetron, 4 mg Orally Dissolving Tablet (ODT)/IV/IO/IM, every 8 hours as needed for nausea or vomiting
interventions.device_specs: 4mg ODT/IV/IO/IM q8h PRN

decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: Repeat doses q30 min prn

decision_thresholds.threshold: Reduction of pain or development of nystagmus (rhythmic eye movement)
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: Endpoints for Analgesia: Reduction of pain or development of nystagmus (rhythmic eye movement)

decision_thresholds.threshold: tolerable pain levels that preserve airway patency, respiratory drive, and mentation
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: The goal of battlefield pain management is to achieve tolerable pain levels that preserve airway patency, respiratory drive, and mentation, rather than pursuing complete pain elimination or total sedation

documentation_requirements: Document a mental status exam using the AVPU method on the DD Form 1380 TCCC Card prior to administering ketamine
important_warnings: Disarm and consider disconnecting communications equipment for casualties who are administered Ketamine
Co-administration of benzodiazepines with Ketamine or Esketamine is NOT recommended
Polypharmacy is not recommended; benzodiazepines should NOT be used in conjunction with opioid analgesia
If casualty appears partially dissociated, safer to administer more ketamine than to use a benzodiazepine

A.name: Antibiotics
A._2026_change: moxifloxacin/ertapenem replaced with cefadroxil PO or ceftriaxone IV/IO/IM

assessment_findings.verbatim: Antibiotics: recommended for all open combat wounds

interventions.name: Cefadroxil PO
interventions.verbatim: Cefadroxil 1 gram PO once a day
interventions.device_specs: 1g PO QD
interventions._2026_change: PO regimen changed from moxifloxacin to cefadroxil

interventions.name: Cephalexin PO (alternative)
interventions.verbatim: Cephalexin 500mg PO every 6 hours (Alternative)
interventions.device_specs: 500mg PO q6h

interventions.name: Ceftriaxone IV/IO/IM
interventions.verbatim: Ceftriaxone 2 gm IV/IO/IM once a day
interventions.device_specs: 2g IV/IO/IM QD
interventions._2026_change: IV regimen changed from ertapenem to ceftriaxone

decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: Antibiotics: recommended for all open combat wounds

decision_thresholds.threshold: PO if able; IV/IO/IM if shock or unconsciousness
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: If unable to take PO meds (shock, unconsciousness): Ceftriaxone 2 gm IV/IO/IM

W.name: Inspect and dress known wounds

assessment_findings.verbatim: Abdominal evisceration – Control bleeding; rinse with clean (and warm if possible) fluid to reduce gross contamination
evidence of ruptured bowel (gastric/intestinal fluid or stool leakage) or active bleeding

interventions.name: wound inspection and dressing
interventions.verbatim: Inspect and dress known wounds

interventions.name: abdominal evisceration coverage
interventions.verbatim: Cover exposed bowel with a moist, sterile dressing or sterile water-impermeable covering
interventions.device_specs: Examples: bowel bag, IV bag, clear food wrap. Secure with adhesive (ioban, chest seal). Transparent preferred to allow re-assessment.

interventions.name: evisceration reduction attempt
interventions.verbatim: If no evidence of bowel leakage and hemorrhage is visibly controlled, a single brief attempt (<60 seconds) may be made to replace/reduce the eviscerated abdominal contents
interventions.device_specs: <60 second single attempt

interventions.name: Ketamine IV/IO for procedural sedation
interventions.verbatim: Ketamine 1-2 mg/kg slow IV/IO push initial dose. Endpoints: procedural (dissociative) anesthesia
interventions.device_specs: 1-2 mg/kg slow IV/IO push
interventions._2026_change: procedural sedation text moved to this section in 2026

interventions.name: Ketamine IM for procedural sedation
interventions.verbatim: Ketamine 300 mg IM (or 2-3 mg/kg IM) initial dose
interventions.device_specs: 300mg IM or 2-3 mg/kg IM

interventions.name: midazolam for emergence phenomenon
interventions.verbatim: If an emergence phenomenon occurs, consider giving 0.5-2 mg IV/IO midazolam
interventions.device_specs: 0.5-2mg IV/IO

decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: a single brief attempt (<60 seconds)

decision_thresholds.threshold: do not attempt if ruptured bowel or active bleeding
decision_thresholds.verbatim_anchor: Reduction: do not attempt if there is evidence of ruptured bowel (gastric/intestinal fluid or stool leakage) or active bleeding

important_warnings: Do NOT FORCE contents back into abdomen or actively bleeding viscera

S.name: Splints
S._note: The 2026 guidelines section 16 contains only a single line. Operational splint vocabulary derives from training materials (SAM splint, traction splint, Sager) referenced elsewhere in the corpus.

assessment_findings.verbatim: Splint fractures and re-check pulses

interventions.name: splint
interventions.verbatim: Splint fractures and re-check pulses

interventions.name: post-splint pulse check
interventions.verbatim: re-check pulses

documentation_mandate.verbatim: Document clinical assessments, treatments rendered, and changes in the casualty's status on a TCCC Card (DD Form 1380). Forward documentation with the casualty to the next level of care.
documentation_mandate._note: This is the load-bearing mandate that ties the entire app to the TCCC rubric. The TCCC Card (DD Form 1380) is named explicitly. This is not an artifact OF TCCC — it is part of the protocol itself.
